item.title: Goldman’s Bitcoin ETF Push Signals Wall Street Taming of Crypto
item.description: Goldman Sachs Group Inc. this week became the latest major Wall Street firm to file for a Bitcoin ETF — joining Morgan Stanley, BlackRock Inc. and a growing roster of institutions racing to package cryptocurrencies for mainstream investors.

item.title: IMF Chief Warns Hasty Central-Bank Action Could Suffocate Growth
item.description: Central banks should resist the urge to hike interest rates in response to the Middle East crisis as this could damage economic output, according to International Monetary Fund Managing Director Kristalina Georgieva.

item.title: AI Pivot Pushes Bitcoin Miners Toward a 70% Revenue Milestone
item.description: The companies that built billion-dollar businesses mining Bitcoin are on track to generate most of their revenue from artificial intelligence by the end of the year — a milestone that marks an entire industry’s pivot away from the cryptocurrency that created it.

item.title: Nagel Echoes Lagarde on Euro Zone Slipping Below ECB Baseline
item.description: The euro-zone economy has shifted beyond the European Central Bank’s base case and toward a more painful outcome due to the Iran war, Governing Council member Joachim Nagel said — echoing an earlier assessment by President Christine Lagarde.

item.title: Kerrygold Butter Maker Sees Iran War Costs Hitting Consumers
item.description: Ireland’s Ornua, the maker of Kerrygold butter, said higher costs resulting from the Middle East conflict are showing up in supply chains, in the latest warning that the war is driving food inflation.

item.title: China Poised to Become Top Tourism Economy as Foreigners Skip US
item.description: China is now on track to become the world’s top tourism economy in the next few years as a sharp drop in foreign visits sets the US back.

item.title: Treasuries Rally Stalls With Yields Near Past Month’s Lows
item.description: The Treasury market rally that sent yields to the lowest closing levels in more than two weeks stalled Wednesday as oil prices steadied with Middle East supply curtailed by the US war on Iran.

item.title: Morgan Stanley Brings High-Grade Bond Deal After Strong Earnings
item.description: Morgan Stanley kicked off an investment-grade bond sale Wednesday, hours after the bank reported its equity traders had a record quarter.

item.title: BOE’s Greene Says Traders Were Right to Trim Rate Hike Bets
item.description: Bank of England policymaker Megan Greene said markets were justified in dialing down bets on interest rates hikes after last month’s surge, speaking in Washington.

item.title: Citadel Securities’ Rubner Sees Lower Volatility Lifting Stocks
item.description: US stocks are poised to benefit from a powerful mix of returning investor flows after the tax season ends, falling volatility, and a positive technical backdrop, according to Citadel Securities.

item.title: After 95% Drop, Brazil’s Worst Stock Is Ready for a Makeover
item.description: Hapvida has fallen a very long way. Shares of the healthcare provider are down 95% from their 2021 peak, wiping out more than $16 billion in market value. Over the last 12 months, it’s the worst-performing stock in Brazil’s benchmark equity index by far.

item.title: UK’s Reeves Says World Is No Safer Since US Attacked Iran
item.description: UK Chancellor of the Exchequer Rachel Reeves said the world is no safer than it was before the US and Israel attacked Iran, in a sign of growing tensions between Donald Trump’s administration and its allies.

item.title: Andreessen, Horowitz Boost AI Super PAC Cash to Over $50 Million
item.description: Venture capitalists Marc Andreessen and Ben Horowitz poured $25 million into a pro-artificial intelligence super political action committee, boosting the industry’s war chest ahead of the November midterm elections.

item.title: Qatar Warns of ‘Huge’ Economic Fallout From War in Coming Months
item.description: Qatar’s finance minister warned of a bigger economic fallout from the Iran war over the coming months if the Strait of Hormuz isn’t reopened soon and trade remains restricted to countries reliant on everything from natural gas and fertilizers to helium.

item.title: HPE Fights Tycoon Mike Lynch’s Widow Over His Estate’s Administrators
item.description: Hewlett Packard Enterprise Co. asked a UK court to name administrators over the estate of British tech entrepreneur Mike Lynch, who died after his luxury yacht sank off the coast of Sicily in 2024, as the technology firm seeks to recoup about $1.2 billion.

item.title: Uzbek Fund Is Said to Hold Talks With Potential IPO Cornerstones
item.description: Uzbekistan’s national investment fund is in talks to add cornerstone investors to its planned London initial public offering, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Nigerian Inflation Quickens, Missing Expectations It Would Cool
item.description: Nigeria’s inflation rate unexpectedly increased in March, stoked by the US-Israeli war on Iran that’s pushed up the cost of fuel.

item.title: Atkins Faces Ticking Clock as He Reshapes Rules for Wall Street
item.description: When Paul Atkins took the helm of Wall Street’s top regulator, he came with a long to-do list: craft rules for the cryptocurrency industry, make initial public offerings “great again” and ease financial reporting for public companies.

item.title: US Homebuilder Sentiment Falls to Seven-Month Low
item.description: US homebuilders’ confidence fell to a seven-month low in April as the Iran war pushed up mortgage rates and materials costs at the start of the spring selling season.

item.title: Double Blockade Keeps Hormuz Shipping at Fraction of Peace Level
item.description: Shipping through the Strait of Hormuz, the waterway that’s become a focal point for the global economy, stayed far below peacetime levels as an effective double blockade stifles vessel movements.

item.title: Allbirds Soars 373% After Sneaker Firm Rebrands as AI Stock
item.description: Allbirds Inc. shares more than quadrupled after the former shoemaker, just days away from ceasing operations, said it would pivot its business to AI compute infrastructure.

item.title: Demand for Colombia LNG Shipments Set to Surge Ahead of El Niño
item.description: Colombian power plants are preparing to ramp up liquefied natural gas imports ahead of an expected El Niño, which would bring dry weather to the Andean country.

item.title: S&P 500 on Pace for Record Close as Ceasefire Rally Continues
item.description: The S&P 500 Index is on track to close at its first record since January, as traders bid up stocks amid optimism over the ceasefire between the US and Iran and robust corporate fundamentals.

item.title: Latvia Premier Ready to Sacrifice Coalition to Rescue Air Baltic
item.description: Latvian Prime Minister Evika Silina said ensuring a €30 million ($35 million) loan for national airline Air Baltic Corp. was more important than the stability of the ruling coalition, six months ahead of parliamentary elections.

item.title: Western Nations Urge Quick Resolution of Middle East Conflict
item.description: A group of Western finance ministers, including from the UK, Japan and Australia, said the Iran war has caused a regrettable loss of life and warned that a prolonged conflict risks derailing the global economy.

item.title: Equinor’s Danske Commodities Cuts 5% of Staff in Strategy Shift
item.description: Equinor’s Danske Commodities A/S has let go about 5% of its staff as part of a strategy shift to focus more on technology-based trading operations.

item.title: Oil-Gear Maker NOV Cuts Earnings Guidance as Iran War Hikes Costs and Snarls Deliveries
item.description: NOV Inc., one of the biggest US makers of oilfield gear, slashed its earnings guidance for the first quarter as the war in the Middle East raises costs and snarls equipment deliveries.

item.title: Putin Demands Officials Explain Why Russian Economy Is Slowing
item.description: President Vladimir Putin ordered government officials to explain why Russia’s economy is slowing, despite his insistence that a downturn must be avoided.

item.title: Gulf Trucking Bypass Moves Metal, Consumer Goods Around Strait
item.description: With the near closure of the Strait of Hormuz in its second month, producers of everything from metal to consumer goods in the Persian Gulf are turning to land transport in an effort to keep their products flowing.

item.title: Morgan Stanley Says Capital Rule Rollback Boosted Trading Haul
item.description: Morgan Stanley’s traders got a boost from President Donald Trump’s deregulation agenda.
